QF.id: IV Strategies Using Interest Rate Derivatives_71

question.text: An insurance company has long-term bond liabilities (pension obligations) with duration of 15 years but can only buy bonds with maximum duration of 10 years. How can ETIRD help?

answer.text: Buy bond futures to synthetically increase the portfolio duration beyond the duration of available bonds
Sell bond futures to reduce the duration gap
Enter into a short straddle to generate premium income for the gap
This situation cannot be addressed using exchange-traded derivatives

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Buying bond futures adds duration to the portfolio synthetically. The combination of physical bonds (duration 10) plus long futures can create an effective portfolio duration closer to the 15-year liability duration. **The Trap:** Selling futures would reduce duration further from the target. Straddles don't address duration matching. ETIRD can help, even if imperfectly. **Cross-Exam Context:** Duration gap management for insurers/pension funds is discussed in Series IV and in advanced fixed income contexts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is ALM (Asset-Liability Management) using derivatives. Insurance companies and pension funds are natural users of long-duration IRD.
